EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong arctic front brought prolonged frigid temperatures to southeast Wyoming Sunday, January 25 through the morning of Monday, January 26. The coldest temperatures occurred Sunday night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Overnight low temperatures ranged from -10 F to -15 F across eastern Laramie County from 1/25 into 1/26. The combination of cold temperatures and gusty winds resulted in a period of extreme wind chills under -25 F. Minimum observed wind chill values reached -31 F at a Union Pacific Railroad sensor (6 S Albin) at 11:05 PM MST on 1/25.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong pressure gradient west of the Laramie Range caused high winds around the Arlington zone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave moving across the Rockies brought snow to the mountains. Precipitation was confined largely to the high terrain with little impacts noticed at lower elevations. The highest snowfall amounts were above 9000 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Peak snowfall report was from Medicine Bow SNOTEL (MBSW4) with 22.6 inches through the event. Several other SNOTELs reported snow accumulations above 8 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong cold front moved across the area during the evening hours of January 15th. Strong winds continued overnight and into the following day. Numerous power outages, damages, and road closures were reported with the abnormally strong northwest winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A maximum wind gust of 85 MPH was observed at the Douglas Airport at 4:27 PM MST on 1/15 with the frontal passage. Multiple sensors across the county recorded strong wind gusts over 60 MPH through the following day. Downed tree branches and power outages were also reported in the city of Douglas. Blowing dust was also reported along Interstate 25 which reduced speeds and visibility on the interstate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of a passing shortwave trough and strong jet stream aloft produced high winds across portions of central Wyoming. The highest wind gusts occurred in the Bighorn Mountains of Johnson County, where the School House RAWS site had a maximum wind gust of 85 mph. The strong wind was accompanied by heavy snow showers at times, bringing reduced visibility and wintry travel conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Willow Creek RAWS site had a maximum wind gust of 70 mph. The Greybull Airport had a wind gust of 63 mph.
